strategy_summary: The binary is a 32-bit ELF with NX enabled and partial RELRO. The vulnerable function reads 256 bytes into a 136-byte buffer, causing a stack overflow. We can use this to overwrite the return address. Since we have the symbols for 'not_called' which calls 'system' with '/bin/date', we can directly jump to 'not_called' to get a shell. We will need to measure the offset to the return address and find a suitable gadget to set up the stack correctly.
